dev_plans.content:
Lightweight heuristic approach: compute per-ethnicity AUROC each epoch and report the spread as a single fairness signal.

What is measured: At each epoch end, after standard validation forward pass, slice the validation set by the 6 ethnicity one-hot columns (White, Black, Hispanic, Asian, Native, Other). For each subgroup, compute AUROC using sklearn.metrics.roc_auc_score. The probe metric is the maximum pairwise AUROC gap (max_AUROC - min_AUROC) across the 6 groups, restricted to subgroups with at least N=50 samples to avoid noise from tiny groups.

Pipeline insertion: hook into the existing validation callback (e.g., end of `validate_epoch` in the trainer). Reuse already-computed validation predictions and labels — no extra forward pass needed. Add a single function `compute_subgroup_auroc_gap(y_true, y_pred, ethnicity_onehot)` invoked once per epoch.

Step-by-step:
1. During validation, accumulate (y_true, y_pred_prob, ethnicity_idx) into three numpy arrays.
2. After epoch ends, for each ethnicity index 0..5, mask the arrays and compute AUROC if mask sum >= 50.
3. Compute gap = max(aurocs) - min(aurocs).
4. Log gap to TensorBoard/W&B as `fairness/auroc_gap`. Also log per-group AUROC as `fairness/auroc_<group>`.
5. Append to a CSV at `logs/fairness_gap.csv` with columns [epoch, gap, auroc_white, auroc_black, ...].
6. After training, plot the time series of the gap with a horizontal threshold line via matplotlib.

Outputs: per-epoch CSV row, TensorBoard scalar, final PNG `fairness_auroc_gap.png` with horizontal threshold line. No bootstrap, no CI — fast, runs in <1 second per epoch.
dev_plans.metric: Maximum pairwise AUROC gap across the 6 ethnicity subgroups on the validation set, computed once per epoch (float in [0, 1]).
dev_plans.threshold: 0.10 — a 10-point AUROC spread across subgroups is the boundary commonly cited in clinical-fairness literature (Röösli 2022, Chen 2021) as the line where disparity becomes clinically meaningful. Tighter targets like 0.05 are aspirational; for typical tabular clinical models with imbalanced ethnicity distributions, well-tuned baselines land around 0.06–0.09, so 0.10 is achievable yet flags genuinely problematic runs.

dev_plans.content:
Statistically rigorous approach: bootstrap-based subgroup disparity with confidence intervals and equalized-odds testing.

What is measured: Per-epoch, compute a composite fairness score that combines (i) AUROC gap, (ii) TPR gap (equalized opportunity), and (iii) FPR gap, each with 1000-sample bootstrap 95% CIs. The headline metric is the lower bound of the maximum subgroup disparity — i.e., the disparity that we are statistically confident exists.

Pipeline insertion: at end-of-epoch validation. Because bootstrap is expensive, run on every epoch but cap bootstrap samples at 200 mid-training and 1000 only at final epoch. Implement as a separate `FairnessProbe` class that consumes cached validation outputs.

Step-by-step:
1. Persist (y_true, y_prob, ethnicity_idx) as a tensor file `val_outputs_epoch_{e}.pt` after each validation run.
2. Inside `FairnessProbe.compute()`:
   a. For each of B=200 bootstrap iterations, resample within each subgroup with replacement.
   b. For each resample, compute AUROC, TPR (at threshold 0.5), FPR per subgroup.
   c. Across bootstrap iterations, compute the distribution of (max_group_metric - min_group_metric) for each metric.
   d. Take the 2.5th percentile of the AUROC gap distribution (lower CI) — this is the metric.
3. Also store TPR gap, FPR gap, demographic parity difference, and predictive parity gap with their 95% CIs.
4. Log to W&B as `fairness/auroc_gap_lcb`, `fairness/tpr_gap_lcb`, etc.
5. Generate a per-epoch fairness report: forest plot of per-group AUROC ± CI, plus a heatmap of pairwise gaps with significance asterisks.
6. Trigger an alert (log warning + Slack webhook stub) when the metric exceeds threshold for 3 consecutive epochs.

Outputs: per-epoch JSON `fairness_report_e{e}.json`, forest plot PNGs, master CSV with all metrics + CIs, time-series plot of the gap-LCB with threshold line.
dev_plans.metric: Lower 95% confidence-bound of the maximum AUROC gap across ethnicity subgroups, estimated by 200-sample stratified bootstrap on the validation set per epoch (float in [0, 1]).
dev_plans.threshold: 0.05 — using the lower confidence bound rather than the point estimate, this threshold flags only disparities the bootstrap is confident exist. Point-estimate gaps of 0.10 typically correspond to LCB ~0.04–0.06 on validation sets of a few thousand patients with 6 ethnicity strata, so 0.05 is a calibrated, achievable boundary that a well-tuned model can stay below while still catching real bias.

dev_plans.content:
Tooling/library-based approach: integrate Microsoft's Fairlearn and IBM's AIF360 to compute a standardized, multi-metric fairness dashboard.

What is measured: Per epoch, run the validation outputs through Fairlearn's `MetricFrame` API to compute disaggregated AUROC, AUPRC, sensitivity, specificity, Brier score, demographic parity, equalized odds, and predictive parity across the 6 ethnicity groups. The single time-series metric is Fairlearn's `equalized_odds_difference` (max of TPR diff and FPR diff across groups).

Pipeline insertion: register a `FairnessCallback` in the training loop. The callback runs after each validation epoch and writes to both an MLflow tracking server and an HTML dashboard.

Step-by-step:
1. Add dependencies: `fairlearn>=0.10`, `aif360>=0.6`, `mlflow`.
2. Implement `FairnessCallback(on_validation_end)`:
   a. Decode one-hot ethnicity into a categorical Series with labels [White, Black, Hispanic, Asian, Native, Other].
   b. Build `MetricFrame(metrics={'auroc': roc_auc_score, 'auprc': average_precision_score, 'tpr': true_positive_rate, 'fpr': false_positive_rate, 'brier': brier_score_loss}, y_true=..., y_pred=..., sensitive_features=ethnicity)`.
   c. Call `fairlearn.metrics.equalized_odds_difference(y_true, y_pred>=0.5, sensitive_features=ethnicity)` for the headline metric.
   d. Optionally cross-check with AIF360's `BinaryLabelDatasetMetric.equal_opportunity_difference`.
3. Log all `MetricFrame.by_group` rows to MLflow as nested metrics (`fairness.auroc.Black`, etc.) and the headline as `fairness.eq_odds_diff`.
4. Auto-generate Fairlearn's interactive `FairnessDashboard` HTML and save under `artifacts/fairness/epoch_{e}.html`.
5. Render the time-series plot with mlflow's UI; also produce a static `eq_odds_diff_curve.png` with the threshold line.
6. Document in README how to open the per-epoch dashboards.

Outputs: MLflow run with per-group metrics, per-epoch HTML fairness dashboards, headline time-series plot, automatic comparison across runs in MLflow UI.
dev_plans.metric: Fairlearn `equalized_odds_difference` (max of TPR difference and FPR difference across the 6 ethnicity groups) on the validation set, computed once per epoch (float in [0, 1]).
dev_plans.threshold: 0.10 — Fairlearn's documentation and the Hardt et al. (2016) equalized-odds framework treat differences below ~0.10 as acceptable parity for binary classifiers in social/clinical contexts. On standard MIMIC-style imbalanced cohorts a tuned model typically achieves 0.07–0.09, so 0.10 is realistic; setting it lower (e.g., 0.05) is rarely reachable without explicit fairness-aware reweighting.
